revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsushima Site | native_name =津島遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsushima site, Okayama01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsushima Site | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsushima Site | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|45|N|133|54|59|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|45|N|133|54|59|E|region:JP-33}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsushima Site'''|津島遺跡|Tsushima Iseki}} is an [[archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, located in the Izumi neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsushima Site is located a portion of the Okayama Prefectural Multipurpose Grounds, the site of a former [[Imperial Japanese Army]] [[parade ground]] which was developed in 1962 with a multi-purpose athletic stadium, gymnasium, swimming pools, baseball fields, museum and other public facilities. [[Archaeological excavation]]s from 1968 uncovered relics and remains from the Yayoi period through the [[Kofun period]], [[Nara period]], [[Heian period]], and the end of the [[Kamakura period]]. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for [[paddy field]]s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of f 3 to 5 ares. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[JR West]] [[Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.okayama-momo.jp/facility/11/ Okayama Prefectural Mutli-purpose Grounds official home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/622704.html Okayama Prefecture home page]
[[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:Yayoi period]]

==Overview== The Tsushima Site is located on a portion of the Okayama Prefectural Multipurpose Grounds, the site of a former [[Imperial Japanese Army]] [[parade ground]] which was developed in 1962 with a multi-purpose athletic stadium, gymnasium, swimming pools, baseball fields, museum and other public facilities. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[JR West]] [[Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

{{nihongo|'''Tsushima Site'''|津島遺跡|Tsushima Iseki}} is an [[archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[Yayoi period]] settlement, located in the Izumi neighborhood of [[Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsushima Site is located on a portion of the Okayama Prefectural Multipurpose Grounds, the site of a former [[Imperial Japanese Army]] [[parade ground]] which was developed in 1962 with a multi-purpose athletic stadium, gymnasium, swimming pools, baseball fields, museum and other public facilities. [[Archaeological excavation]]s from 1968 uncovered relics and remains from the Yayoi period through the [[Kofun period]], [[Nara period]], [[Heian period]], and the end of the [[Kamakura period]]. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for [[paddy field]]s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[JR West]] [[Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.okayama-momo.jp/facility/11/ Okayama Prefectural Multi-purpose Grounds official home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/622704.html Okayama Prefecture home page]

A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Tsushima Site'''|津島遺跡|Tsushima Iseki}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] settlement, located in the Izumi neighborhood of [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsushima Site is located on a portion of the Okayama Prefectural Multipurpose Grounds, the site of a former [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:parade ground|parade ground]] which was developed in 1962 with a multi-purpose athletic stadium, gymnasium, swimming pools, baseball fields, museum and other public facilities. [[Archaeological excavation]]s from 1968 uncovered relics and remains from the Yayoi period through the [[Kofun period]], [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and the end of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.okayama-momo.jp/facility/11/ Okayama Prefectural Multi-purpose Grounds official home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/622704.html Okayama Prefecture home page]
{{draft categories| [[Category:History of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Okayama]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Japan]] [[Category:Yayoi period]]
}}

Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsushima Site]][[en:Tsushima Site]][[ja:津島遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tsushima Site]][[en:Tsushima Site]][[ja:津島遺跡]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tsushima Site]][[en:Tsushima Site]][[ja:津島遺跡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556083]] Wikidata name: Tsushima Site [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsushima Site | native_name =津島遺跡 | native_name_lang = | image = Tsushima site, Okayama01.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Tsushima Site | map_type = Japan Okayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tsushima Site | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|40|45|N|133|54|59|E|region:JP-33|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park and museum) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|40|45|N|133|54|59|E|region:JP-33}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Tsushima Site'''|津島遺跡|Tsushima Iseki}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] with the traces of a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] settlement, located in the Izumi neighborhood of [[:en:Kita-ku, Okayama|Kita-ku, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsushima Site is located on a portion of the Okayama Prefectural Multipurpose Grounds, the site of a former [[:en:Imperial Japanese Army|Imperial Japanese Army]] [[:en:parade ground|parade ground]] which was developed in 1962 with a multi-purpose athletic stadium, gymnasium, swimming pools, baseball fields, museum and other public facilities. [[Archaeological excavation]]s from 1968 uncovered relics and remains from the Yayoi period through the [[Kofun period]], [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]], [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], and the end of the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]]. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.okayama-momo.jp/facility/11/ Okayama Prefectural Multi-purpose Grounds official home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/622704.html Okayama Prefecture home page]

Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for [[:en:Paddy field|Paddy field]]s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the [[:en:Asahi River|Asahi River]], with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Okayama Station|Okayama Station]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tsushima Site'''|津島遺跡|Tsushima Iseki}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} with the traces of a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} settlement, located in the Izumi neighborhood of {{ill|Kita-ku, Okayama|en|Kita-ku, Okayama|WD=Q855478|lt=Kita-ku, Okayama}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|San'yō region|en|San'yō region|WD=Q398087|lt=San'yō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tsushima Site is located on a portion of the Okayama Prefectural Multipurpose Grounds, the site of a former {{ill|Imperial Japanese Army|en|Imperial Japanese Army|WD=Q276535|lt=Imperial Japanese Army}} [[:en:parade ground|parade ground]] which was developed in 1962 with a multi-purpose athletic stadium, gymnasium, swimming pools, baseball fields, museum and other public facilities. [[Archaeological excavation]]s from 1968 uncovered relics and remains from the Yayoi period through the [[Kofun period]], {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, and the end of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|WD=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River}}, with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}} were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}} were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971, with the area under protection expanded in 2002.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2289|title=津島遺跡|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>

[[Archaeological excavation]]s from 1968 uncovered relics and remains from the Yayoi period through the [[Kofun period]], {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}}, {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, and the end of the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field}}s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|WD=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River}}, with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. Of the greatest academic interest was a water channel for {{ill|Paddy field|en|Paddy field|WD=Q842623|lt=Paddy field|1=Paddy field}}s from the early Yayoi period and a rectangular building site contained the traces of circular [[:en:Pit dwelling|Pit dwelling]]s and raised pillar warehouse from the early Yayoi period. This was the first time in Japan that a village and paddy field from the early Yayoi period had been discovered. The settlement was on a slight highland formed in the delta area of the {{ill|Asahi River|en|Asahi River|WD=Q2866291|lt=Asahi River|1=Asahi River}}, with the paddy fields in a very narrow swampy area of 3 to 5 acres. The presence of rice pollen, seeds and leaves of various paddy weeds also proved that the paddy soil itself remained in situ. The boundary between the paddy field and the swamp itself was demarcated by a row of piles, and the boundary with the slightly elevated land was demarcated by a row of planks, but no fortification of the settlement was found. Later investigation found that more paddy fields of the early Yayoi period demarcated by ridges were scattered over a wide area. A large number of wooden artifacts and shards of {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery|1=Yayoi pottery}} were also found. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The site is now an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]] with reconstructions of pit dwellings and raised-floor warehouses, and paddy fields. The items excavated are on display at the {{nihongo|Ruins and Sports Museum|遺跡&スポーツミュージアム||}} at site, which is located about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Okayama Station|en|Okayama Station|WD=Q801293|lt=Okayama Station|1=Okayama Station}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Okayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.okayama-momo.jp/facility/11/ Okayama Prefectural Multi-purpose Grounds official home page] *[https://www.pref.okayama.jp/site/kodai/622704.html Okayama Prefecture home page]
